messages.content: These risk write-ups share funnel target nodes, which does NOT make them the same risk — a funnel is coarse, so mechanistically distinct risks (a certification clock, a grid-connection queue, a distributor line-review cycle) can all compile to the same node. PARTITION the cluster into groups of TRUE duplicates: same underlying mechanism, same causal story, such that presenting both to a client would read as repetition. Distinct risks come back as singleton groups (absorbs: []). For a genuine duplicate group, pick the best exemplar (sharpest mechanism, best evidence) as keepId and fold the absorbed write-ups' UNIQUE early-warning indicators into it. Over-merging destroys distinct insights — when in doubt, keep them separate.

content: {"rationale":"The cluster contains several mechanistically distinct risks despite shared funnel targets. I identify these true-duplicate groups: (1) The SOM window-semantics / benchmark period-mismatch risks — three write-ups all argue the 1% is a multi-year cumulative figure read as a Year-1 snapshot, citing the same Prospeo/IdeaPlan/Wise/TryBuildCo/Tractian evidence. (2) The pre-let/framework lockout risks — write-ups arguing new-build electrical BOMs and pre-let frameworks close the Year-1 contestable pool; there are multiple angles (general pre-let lockout, 83% FLAP-D pre-let, framework agreements closing the 40% large-operator cell, and the AVL-qualification-plus-framework foreclosure). These split into two mechanisms: (a) framework-agreement/AVL foreclosure of the large-operator cell specifically, and (b) design-freeze/pre-let lockout of new-build spend. (3) Bundle-pricing killing bake-offs is distinct — cross-subsidised zero-margin PDU pricing inside power+cooling packages. Distinct singletons: construction plateau (AI-capex digestion), RED/EN18031 wireless-radio cert gate, CRA conformity clock, firmware/security audit gate, PoC acceptance reversal base rate, Frankfurt grid queue, and Frankfurt concentration×timing. I keep the framework-foreclosure and AVL-qualification risks separate from pure design-freeze pre-let lockout because one is about buyer-side procurement contracts/qualification and the other about the timing of new-build spec-lock, though they overlap heavily. On balance, framework/AVL foreclosure of the large-operator cell (two write-ups) merge, and the two pre-let-lockout new-build-timing write-ups merge, and the structure-independence Frankfurt-cycle write-up is a distinct concentration×timing framing but mechanistically it duplicates the pre-let lockout applied to the DE×large cell — I keep it separate as it emphasizes the correlation/concentration argument rather than the pre-let contract mechanism. The two regulatory cert gates (RED, CRA) and the firmware/security audit gate are three different regulatory mechanisms and stay separate.","groups":[{"keepId":"risk.demand-discontinuity.construction-plateau-lumpy-year1","absorbs":[],"foldedIndicators":[]},{"keepId":"risk.execution-window.som-window-semantics-year1-zero","absorbs":["risk.base-rate-analogy.som-window-cumulative-misread"],"foldedIndicators":[{"signal":"Quote-to-PO conversion time in the first CE opportunities","where":"Own CRM pipeline reviewed at month 6; cross-referenced against TED award timelines for rack-power line items","threshold":"Median cycle >9 months at month 6","updates":"increases"}]},{"keepId":"risk.competitive-foreclosure.framework-agreements-close-first-buyer","absorbs":["risk.regulatory-gauntlet.avl-framework-qualification-forecloses-large-operator-cell"],"foldedIndicators":[{"signal":"AVL qualification requirements and cycle times quoted in operator supplier-onboarding portals","where":"Equinix/Digital Realty/NTT GDC supplier registration portals; Vantage/CyrusOne EU procurement contacts","threshold":"Mandatory ISO 27001 or 12+ month qualification stated for electrical-fit-out vendors","updates":"increases"}]},{"keepId":"risk.execution-window.prelet-pipeline-lockout","absorbs":["risk.demand-discontinuity.prelet-lockout-year1-contestability"],"foldedIndicators":[{"signal":"FLAP-D pre-let percentage and vacancy in successive half-year prints","where":"JLL EMEA Data Centre Report and Cushman & Wakefield EMEA updates","threshold":"Pre-let stays above 80% with vacancy below 8%","updates":"increases"}]},{"keepId":"risk.regulatory-gauntlet.red-en18031-wireless-sensor-gate","absorbs":[],"foldedIndicators":[]},{"keepId":"risk.execution-window.firmware-security-audit-gate","absorbs":[],"foldedIndicators":[]},{"keepId":"risk.base-rate-analogy.poc-acceptance-reversal","absorbs":[],"foldedIndicators":[]},{"keepId":"risk.regulatory-gauntlet.cra-conformity-clock-outlasts-yam-window","absorbs":[],"foldedIndicators":[]},{"keepId":"risk.competitive-foreclosure.bundle-pricing-kills-bakeoffs","absorbs":[],"foldedIndicators":[]},{"keepId":"risk.demand-discontinuity.frankfurt-grid-queue-de-slip","absorbs":[],"foldedIndicators":[]},{"keepId":"risk.structure-independence.frankfurt-cycle-lockout-year1","absorbs":[],"foldedIndicators":[]}]}
